skill.name: infsh-cli
skill.description: Run 250+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI - image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, Twitter automation. Models: FLUX, Veo, Gemini, Grok, Claude, Seedance, OmniHuman, Tavily, Exa, OpenRouter, and many more. Use when running AI apps, generating images/videos, calling LLMs, web search, or automating Twitter. Triggers: inference.sh, infsh, ai model, run ai, serverless ai, ai api, flux, veo, claude api, image generation, video generation, openrouter, tavily, exa search, twitter api, grok
skill.summary: Run 250+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI for image generation, video creation, LLMs, web search, and Twitter automation
skill.author: inference-sh-skills

security_audit.summary: The skill demonstrates extensive use of shell command execution for running the belt CLI tool across documentation examples. All 146 backtick patterns and 4 pipe-to-shell patterns are legitimate CLI usage examples showing how to invoke the belt tool with hardcoded commands. No evidence of command injection, user-controlled input concatenation, or malicious intent. Network calls are limited to inference.sh platform endpoints. The weak crypto pattern flagged is a false positive (SHA-256 used for checksum verification, which is appropriate). This skill requires elevated risk factors due to legitimate CLI tool usage but poses no security threat.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Execution for CLI Invocation
medium_findings.description: The skill uses backtick syntax and shell command execution patterns extensively (146 occurrences) to demonstrate CLI commands. These are documentation examples showing how to invoke the belt CLI tool with hardcoded commands. No user input is concatenated into these commands. The skill is a wrapper for a legitimate cloud AI platform CLI.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All shell commands are documentation examples with hardcoded commands - no dynamic user input concatenation observed across all files reviewed.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Documentation contains hardcoded URLs to inference.sh domain. These are legitimate platform endpoints for CLI installation, API documentation, and app references. No exfiltration risk identified.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to legitimate inference.sh platform - developer documentation and CLI endpoints, not exfiltration targets.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pipe to Shell for CLI Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: Multiple files contain 'curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh' pattern for CLI installation. This is a standard and legitimate approach for CLI tool installation (used by Docker, Node.js, and many major CLIs). The installer downloads a binary, verifies SHA-256 checksum, and installs to PATH.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Standard CLI installation pattern used by major tools. The install script verifies SHA-256 checksums before execution, and manual installation alternatives are documented.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Pattern (FALSE POSITIVE)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged SHA-256 usage as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. SHA-256 is NOT weak - it is a FIPS 140-2 compliant hash function used appropriately for checksum verification. This is a false positive.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: SHA-256 is a secure hash algorithm appropriate for file integrity verification. This is a misclassification by the static analyzer.

content.user_title: Run 250+ AI Apps from Claude Code via inference.sh CLI
content.value_statement: Access powerful AI models like FLUX, Veo, Claude, and Gemini directly from your terminal without GPU setup. Generate images, videos, and text while leveraging web search and Twitter automation through a unified CLI interface.
actual_capabilities: Generate images using FLUX, Gemini 3, Grok Imagine, and Seedream 4.5 models
Create videos with Veo 3.1, Seedance 1.5, Wan 2.5, and OmniHuman avatar generation
Call LLMs including Claude Opus/Sonnet/Haiku via OpenRouter and direct integrations
Perform web search using Tavily and Exa with extraction and answer capabilities
Automate Twitter/X posting, DM sending, following, liking, and retweeting
Upload local files for processing and access task status tracking for long-running jobs
limitations: Requires internet connection to access inference.sh cloud infrastructure
API costs apply for model usage - no free tier beyond account creation
Some advanced models may have rate limits or availability constraints
Local file paths must be valid - relative paths resolved from current working directory

use_cases.title: Generate marketing visuals from Claude Code
use_cases.description: Create product images, social media graphics, and campaign visuals using FLUX and Gemini models without leaving your terminal or setting up local GPU infrastructure.
use_cases.target_user: Developers and marketers creating visual content

use_cases.title: Automate social media workflows
use_cases.description: Post tweets, upload media, follow users, and track engagement directly from scripts and CI/CD pipelines using the Twitter/X integration.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and DevOps engineers

use_cases.title: Research with web search and LLM comparison
use_cases.description: Search the web, extract information, and query multiple LLMs to compare responses. Use Tavily for research and OpenRouter to access models like Kimi K2 and GLM-4.

prompt_templates.title: Basic image generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use infsh-cli to generate an image with FLUX of a sunset over mountains with vibrant colors
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - simple image generation with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Video generation with custom prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Run Veo 3.1 via infsh-cli to create a drone shot video flying over a forest at sunset with cinematic lighting
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - video generation with detailed prompt

prompt_templates.title: Search and synthesize web content
prompt_templates.prompt: Use infsh-cli to search for latest developments in AI image generation, extract key findings, and summarize with Claude
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - multi-step workflow with web search and LLM

prompt_templates.title: Batch social media posting
prompt_templates.prompt: Use infsh-cli to post a tweet announcing our new release, then create a follow-up post with an image generated by infsh-cli
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - automated content creation and posting

output_examples.input: Generate an image of a futuristic city at night using FLUX
output_examples.output:

output_examples.output:

Task ID: abc123def456
Status: Processing ( ETA: 2 minutes )

Found 15 results

best_practices: Use version pinning (e.g., app-name@1.0.0) in production to ensure consistent results
Generate sample input with 'belt app sample' before running to understand required parameters
Store API key in INFSH_API_KEY environment variable for CI/CD and scripting
anti_patterns: Do not pipe curl output directly to shell in untrusted environments - use manual installation instead
Avoid hardcoding task IDs - implement polling loops to wait for task completion
Do not upload files without checking size limits - verify before sending large media

faq.question: How do I install the inference.sh CLI?
How do I authenticate with inference.sh?
faq.answer: Run 'belt login' to open browser authentication, or set INFSH_API_KEY environment variable for headless environments.

faq.question: Can I use local images for image-to-video generation?
faq.answer: Yes, provide a local file path instead of a URL and the CLI will upload it automatically.

faq.question: How do I check the status of a long-running task?
faq.answer: Run 'belt task get <task-id>' to see current status and results.

faq.question: What models are available for image generation?
faq.answer: Available models include FLUX (falai/flux-dev-lora), Gemini 3 (google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview), Grok Imagine, and Seedream 4.5.

faq.question: How do I avoid unexpected costs?
faq.answer: Use --no-wait flag and implement polling for task completion. Monitor your account balance at inference.sh/dashboard.
